symptoms.symptom_name: New sharply-demarcated hot, erythematous, tender plaque on the shin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A demarcated hot, red, tender plaque with fever is the classic appearance of cellulitis.
A surface demarcated hot plaque is a skin infection pattern rather than a deep diffuse DVT.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic 5-year non-pitting hyperkeratotic unilateral swelling (lymphoedema) with recurrent prior cellulitis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lymphatic compromise predisposes to recurrent rapidly-progressive cellulitis.
A low-grade fever without systemic toxicity supports localised cellulitis amenable to oral antibiotics.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-pitting hyperkeratotic swelling with no varicosities (lymphatic, not venous)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of varicosities/haemosiderin with a non-pitting hyperkeratotic pattern favours lymphoedema over venous disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Localised surface hot plaque, not a diffuse deep calf ache (argues against DVT)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A localised surface infection pattern rather than a deep diffuse ache/swelling argues against DVT (though it should be excluded).

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive Stemmer's sign, peau d'orange, hyperkeratosis and Doppler-only pulses on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A positive Stemmer's sign with hyperkeratotic non-pitting oedema is characteristic of lymphoedema.

symptoms.symptom_name: Delayed lymphatic transit on lymphoscintigraphy (chronic lymphoedema)

investigation_details.expected_result: Delayed lymphatic transit confirming lymphoedema; venous duplex to exclude DVT if clinically uncertain
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Lymphatic diagnosis remains unconfirmed; the acute priority is treating the cellulitis and excluding DVT clinically.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Delayed lymphatic transit on lymphoscintigraphy confirms lymphoedema.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A new demarcated hot tender plaque with low-grade fever on a chronically lymphoedematous limb, in a patient with recurrent prior cellulitis, indicates acute infection needing prompt in-person antibiotics and DVT exclusion, with sepsis safety-netting.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical assessment with erythema marking
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms cellulitis, defines extent (marking the leading edge to monitor spread), and guides antibiotic choice.
Leg change on a swollen limb warrants excluding DVT before attributing all to cellulitis.
Assess severity and systemic involvement if features of sepsis emerge.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Self-starting leftover home antibiotics without assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Risks inappropriate antibiotic choice/dose, masks a possible DVT, and delays proper assessment.
